name: Strike-Trooper
cpNote: Orbital Strike (3 CP, 75px radius, 150 dmg base / 172 Terran-equivalent) can be called via canvas click when CP≥3. Accord CP regen: 1 per 26 seconds (standard 1/30s). Max CP: 10. Starting CP: 3.
physical: Identical white-and-gold composite armor covering every centimeter. Segmented at joints with amber polymer mesh. Featureless helmet with horizontal amber visor tied to Tactical Link HUD. No visible individual features — the individual is the network. Pulse Rifle folds from shoulder mount in 0.4 seconds: three-round bursts of compressed amber plasma at near-light velocity. Personal Marker Drone hovers above each squad — disc-shaped autonomous platform providing real-time targeting data and relay.
narrative: The Accord's philosophy made flesh: the individual soldier is a NODE in a network. A single trooper is less dangerous than a single Shock-Marine. But each hit Marks the target for every Accord unit in range. Thirty mediocre shooters become one surgical targeting laser. The first shot might miss. The second hits. From that moment, every subsequent shot from every Accord unit lands with uncanny precision.

name: Marker-Drone
physical: Three large disc-shaped drones in triangular formation at chest height. Each half-meter diameter with central amber sensor eye rotating 360°. Active Marker Light projectors paint targets with visible amber beam. Encrypted micro-burst communication relays to all Accord units in range. Silent. No personalities. Tools.
narrative: The Tactical Link's primary force multiplier. Active designation provides +15% ADDITIONAL accuracy on top of standard Mark — combined +30% against any designated target. Against a Commander unit it is effectively a death sentence. Cannot defend itself — fragile, unarmed, must be protected by frontline units. Losing the drone squad significantly degrades targeting capability for the entire formation.

name: Crisis-Mech
physical: 3.5m humanoid mech in white-gold composite. Lighter and faster than any comparable walker — built for rapid repositioning rather than frontal combat. Modular weapon hardpoints on each arm: burst cannon, missile pod, or any Accord weapon configuration. Fluid organic movement — can sprint, leap 5m on jump jets (amber flare from leg vents, 2–3 second boost), and brace for firing in one continuous sequence.
narrative: The one Accord unit designed to break from rigid ranged doctrine and ADAPT. Plugs flanks. Jet-jumps to elevated firing positions. Engages melee breaches with burst cannon fire. Pilots come from the Warmth Caste — the closest thing to individual heroes in the Accord order of battle, given personal discretion. That freedom is both their greatest strength and potential vulnerability.

name: Hammerhead Hover-Tank
physical: Sleek low-profile hover-tank on repulsor fields. Angular wedge of white composite deflecting both projectiles and sensor returns. Heavy railgun fires tungsten penetrators at Mach 8. 0.3-second charge cycle — blue-white glow along the barrel, then a flat sharp CRACK more electrical than mechanical. The penetrator delivers catastrophic kinetic transfer: armor shatters, bulkheads buckle, spines snap.
narrative: The Accord's solution to both heavy armor AND the Core Guardian problem. The kinetic railgun bypasses energy absorption through raw physical impact — no field to absorb, no plasma to deflect. Most accurate ground-based anti-armor platform in any faction's inventory. Tactical Link feeds fire control accounting for distance, wind, target movement, and planetary rotation. First-round hits at extreme range are routine. Two shots to destroy a Siege-Breaker. Three to hull a War-God Idol.

name: Broadside-Battlesuit
physical: Six-meter powered exosuit — pilot interfaces through neural feedback harness. Wider than it is tall: twin heavy railgun accelerators (left shoulder), smart missile system (right), integrated sensor array (chest and helmet). Cannot move quickly. Stabilizers so precise it fires at full accuracy while walking. Both railguns fire synchronized: two tungsten penetrators arriving at the same impact point simultaneously. Pilot visible through a narrow visor slit — tiny face dwarfed by weapon systems.
narrative: The thesis made manifest: precision through overwhelming technology. Dominates an entire sector — synchronized volleys every 4 seconds. Cannot flee, cannot operate at close quarters. A FIREBASE — the entire Accord ground doctrine revolves around protecting it. Against Core Guardians: the most valuable unit on the field. Kinetic damage bypasses energy absorption entirely. A pair of Broadsides with Marker-Drone support systematically dismantles Guardian positions.

name: Razorshark Fighter
physical: Angular single-seat fighter with internal weapon bays. Electromagnetic drive — nearly silent, subsonic hum and faint amber glow. Burst cannons fire from bays opening for fractions of a second. Tactical Link integration allows fire control to synchronize with ground-based Marks for devastatingly accurate aerial engagement.
narrative: Silent precision. Once an enemy aircraft is tagged by any Accord unit, the Razorshark's fire control tracks with near-perfect accuracy. The enemy rarely hears it coming.

name: Sun-Shark Bomber
physical: Twin-engine bomber carrying guided pulse bombs — each individually targeted through Tactical Link data. Single bombing run eliminates 3–5 Marked high-value ground targets with pinpoint accuracy, leaving the surrounding area untouched.
narrative: Against unmarked targets: significantly less effective. Precision doctrine depends entirely on Tactical Link data flowing before the strike. Without active Marks, it is just an expensive bomber with no particular advantage. Mark first. Then send the Sun-Shark.

name: Sun-Chanter
physical: Hovering platform broadcasting a wide-band amplification field. Extends Tactical Link effective range +50% in all directions. Mark expiration timer 8→12 seconds. No offensive weapons whatsoever — pure network support. Must be escorted at all times.
narrative: Transforms the targeting network from effective to OPPRESSIVE. Marks persist longer, propagate further, accumulate faster. Destroying it significantly degrades the entire force's combat effectiveness — it is always the first target a competent enemy designates.

name: Manta-Dropship
physical: Largest Accord transport — carries an entire combined-arms company including vehicles and one Crisis-Mech. Rear cargo ramp deploys in 10 seconds. HEAVILY armed: twin chin-mounted railgun turrets, smart missile wing pods. Suppresses the landing zone while troops deploy — a self-contained combined-arms operation.
narrative: Unlike any other faction's transport, the Manta can fight with purpose. Hovers over the landing zone providing serious fire support through the full deployment phase. Drop troops and maintain suppression simultaneously.

name: Solar-Eclipse
physical: Massive aerial platform with Nova Charge launcher — compressed plasma spheres detonating in 15m radius. Four charges in rapid succession — overlapping plasma detonations deny terrain, destroy fortifications, and annihilate exposed infantry.
narrative: Against Core Guardians: counterproductive — plasma energy is absorbed and heals them. Must switch to kinetic bomb payloads, sacrificing devastating area denial for physical damage. An expensive compromise.

name: Flare-Dart
physical: Tiny lightning-fast single-pilot interceptor. Light pulse cannons deal minimal damage. Purpose: get close, Mark the target with Tactical Link, get out. A squadron of Flare-Darts racing ahead tags every enemy ship in the engagement window with glowing amber lock signatures.
narrative: The opening move of every Accord space engagement. Establishes first Marks enabling the main fleet's devastating alpha strike. Without Flare-Darts the fleet is effective. With them it is terrifying.

name: Sun-Chariot
physical: Medium frigate carrying BOTH pulse cannons AND railgun turrets — the first Accord vessel with dual weapon types. Energy systems for conventional engagements, kinetic for Core Guardians. Tactical Link integration makes it the fleet's universal platform.
narrative: Can Mark, screen, and provide anti-Guardian kinetic fire within the same engagement. No single specialization, but no critical gap either. The all-purpose escort.

name: Emissary-Cruiser
physical: Dual-purpose vessel: heavy combat ship AND diplomatic platform. Ion cannon batteries plus railgun arrays for combat. Conference chambers, translator systems, secure communication arrays for negotiation. In fleet: unlocks ENHANCED diplomatic options — broader dialogue trees, higher persuasion thresholds, audiences with factions that would otherwise refuse.
narrative: A solid combatant with a unique diplomatic capability no other faction's capital offers. The Accord's philosophy stated plainly: negotiate from a position of overwhelming firepower. The Emissary-Cruiser is both the position and the firepower.

name: Custodian-Carrier
physical: Large carrier housing a Tactical Link Core — massive computational array amplifying the targeting network fleet-wide. +5% accuracy to all Accord ships while the Core is operational. Launches 30+ fighters in rapid waves. Backed by Flare-Dart squadrons: every enemy ship in the engagement is Marked within 30 seconds of combat opening.
narrative: Destroying the Custodian removes the fleet-wide accuracy bonus and disrupts fighter replenishment — it is always the highest-priority enemy target. The Accord's opening sequence: wave of interceptors tagging everything, followed immediately by main fleet alpha strike with full Mark bonuses active.

name: Corona-Titan
physical: Massive angular capital ship built around the Sun Cannon — a stellar energy focusing array that captures and concentrates the nearest star's radiation. 15-second charge cycle (hull glows from white to blinding amber). Then 3 seconds of sustained stellar fire that bisects capital ships. Visible from planetary surfaces as a golden line drawn across the void. 60-second cooldown. Between shots: conventional broadside ion and railgun batteries.
narrative: The thesis made physical: technology, precision, and overwhelming calculated force. Doesn't brawl, endure, or dodge. It points at a problem, charges, and makes it cease to exist. Clean. Efficient. Optimal. Whether 'optimal' is enough when the galaxy is burning is the player's question to answer.
